title: Ransomware Incident Response
description: Response playbook for a suspected or confirmed ransomware event on endpoints or servers, where files are being encrypted or a ransom note has appeared. Prioritizes isolation and evidence preservation over hasty cleanup.
author: Cognis Digital

steps.name: Verify offline backups are recoverable
steps.description: Confirm that immutable or offline backups exist for critical systems and that a recent restore has been test-validated.

steps.name: Pre-authorize network isolation
steps.description: Document who may approve segment-level isolation and ensure the response team can reach switch and firewall consoles out-of-band.

steps.name: Confirm encryption activity
steps.description: Validate that mass file modification or a ransom note is genuine and not a false alarm.
steps.detection: Endpoint telemetry showing rapid file renames or extension changes, plus alerts on canary files in monitored shares.

steps.name: Scope lateral movement
steps.description: Identify how the actor spread between hosts to bound the affected set.
steps.detection: Authentication logs for unusual administrative logons and SMB sessions between workstations that do not normally communicate.

steps.name: Isolate affected hosts
steps.response: Network-isolate encrypting hosts immediately while keeping them powered on to preserve volatile evidence; do not wipe.

steps.name: Disable abused accounts and shares
steps.response: Disable the compromised service or admin accounts and remove the share permissions the actor used to traverse.

steps.name: Remove persistence and tooling
steps.response: Identify and remove the actor's persistence mechanisms and dropped tooling from all affected hosts before rebuild.

steps.name: Rebuild compromised systems
steps.response: Reimage affected endpoints and servers from known-good media rather than attempting in-place cleanup.

steps.name: Restore from validated backups
steps.response: Restore data from verified clean backups onto rebuilt systems, restoring the most critical business services first.

steps.name: Monitor for reinfection
steps.response: Watch restored systems closely for re-encryption attempts or returning indicators before declaring the incident closed.

steps.name: Document timeline and close gaps
steps.description: Reconstruct the intrusion timeline, record the initial access vector, and prioritize the control gaps that allowed spread.
